topic: Introducing Vuelmate, a brand-new open-source Vue 3 component library that provides warm, accessible UI patterns out of the box for rapid prototyping.
audience: Frontend developers at a Vue.js meetup
outline_hint: Why We Built Vuelmate - The gap in accessible Vue 3 UI libraries
Core Philosophy: Warm & Accessible - Design principles and gradient defaults
Setup and Installation - Quick start with Vite and npm
Live Demo: Building a Dashboard - Combining layout and data components
Vuelmate vs. Existing Libraries - Feature comparison and bundle size
Customizing Gradients and Themes - Using CSS variables and utility classes
Open Source Roadmap - Upcoming components and community calls
Q&A and Community Links - GitHub repo and Discord invite

topic: A step-by-step hands-on lab demonstrating how to build BoardSync, a real-time collaborative whiteboard app using Vue 3, Pinia, and WebSockets.
audience: Intermediate JS developers attending a community workshop

topic: Reviewing the migration of the Lumina Design System from React to Vue 3, focusing on warm gradient tokens and component parity for the new frontend stack.
outline_hint: Lumina UI 2.0: Why Move to Vue? - Ecosystem alignment and reactivity benefits
Project Scope & Timeline - Phased rollout over Q3
Gradient Token Architecture - New warm color system for modern UI
React vs. Vue Component Parity Table - Tracking completed and pending mappings
Migration Strategy & Risk Assessment - Phased approach and breaking changes
Demo: Interactive Storybook - Live preview of Vue components
Beta Feedback Summary - Developer pain points and resolutions
Next Steps & Launch Plan - V2 release date and upcoming workshops

topic: Announcing Slidev 5.0 at the VueConf opening keynote, showcasing new motion-powered transitions, native Vite integration, and speaker mode enhancements.
